finding.contract_claim: getNodeLabel accesses node.children (line 18) and node.size/node.extension (line 21) on a value typed FsNode = FileNode | DirectoryNode, and each property exists on only one union member — accessing the wrong variant would yield undefined at runtime.
finding.observed_behavior: Each access is guarded by a discriminant check on node.type (line 17): the `directory` branch reads .children and the post-guard fall-through is narrowed to FileNode before reading .size/.extension.
finding.triage_reasoning:
Reality: The code is correctly guarded. Line 17 checks `node.type === 'directory'`, which TypeScript uses as a discriminant to narrow `FsNode` to `DirectoryNode` inside the branch — making `.children` safe. After the branch exits, TypeScript narrows the remaining type to `FileNode`, making `.size` and `.extension` safe at line 21. The same pattern is repeated in `collectFiles` at lines 25-29.

The claim alleges 'accessing the wrong variant would yield undefined at runtime', but every access is preceded by the exact discriminant check that prevents cross-variant access. No bug exists.
